name: code-review-cycle
description: SDLC code review phase: treat a diff (or changed-files list) as the review topic, run 4 adversarial personas (senior-dev / security-auditor / red-team / maintainability-architect) via a2a_fanout + cross_critique, and persist surviving concerns to dissent-log.json so the reviewer role can triage.
_migration_note: v2: Typed Flow format (role/produces/consumes). v1 used type only (no channel data flow — all communication via file paths in context). v2.1: added a fourth persona (Staff Engineer — Maintainability & Architecture Fit) so non-functional concerns (maintainability, extensibility, operability) get a dedicated lens instead of being an afterthought under 'Senior Developer'. Add a 'UX Reviewer' persona in `context.personas` for missions that touch a user-facing surface (Chronos panels, CLI UX, etc.) — not included by default since most self-ops changes have no UI surface.

context.review_topic: Review the changes below for correctness, security, and risk.
personas: Staff Engineer — Maintainability & Architecture Fit (evaluate long-term maintainability, extensibility, operability/observability, and whether the change fits or fights the existing architecture)
